title: Launch Campaign Prompt Studio
subtitle: Built for founders, creators, and marketers coordinating a real launch. Turn the offer, dates, proof, and channels into campaign decisions, copy briefs, and a launch manual your team can run.

profile.label: What you sell and who buys it
profile.placeholder: Example: a $3,000 operations advisory package for owner-led manufacturers with 20 to 100 employees
profile.helper: One or two sentences. Every prompt in this studio uses this.

profile.label: Your buyer in their own words
profile.placeholder: Example: 'We keep fixing the same fire every Friday' is how clients describe the problem
profile.helper: Use phrases real buyers or clients say.

profile.label: The offer or project you're working on now
profile.placeholder: Example: rebuilding the spring group program before enrollment opens

profile.label: Your main goal for the next 90 days
profile.placeholder: Example: enroll 12 right-fit clients without adding more sales calls

profile.label: Voice, proof, or constraints the AI should respect
profile.placeholder: Example: plainspoken, no income claims, two-person delivery team

categories.title: Launch Direction
categories.note: Lock the buyer, promise, offer, and target before building assets.

categories.title: Offer and Proof
categories.note: Build a buying case the campaign can support honestly.

categories.title: Campaign Map
categories.note: Give every date and channel a clear job.

categories.title: Launch Assets
categories.note: Turn the campaign argument into pages, emails, events, and posts.

categories.title: Control Room
categories.note: Read the launch while there is still time to act.

categories.title: Review and Improve
categories.note: Stress-test launch material before buyers do.

workflows.title: Launch Direction Memo
workflows.description: Set the audience, offer, central promise, conversion goal, dates, and nonnegotiable choices.
workflows.role: launch strategist responsible for the commercial target, central argument, and campaign tradeoffs, with direct responsibility for the launch direction memo this prompt produces

fields.label: Describe the offer, audience, dates, and launch target
fields.placeholder: Example: open a 12-seat advisory cohort from September 9 to 16 to a 6,000-person email list
fields.helper: Give the facts the output should use. Specifics beat background.

fields.label: Numbers, evidence, or limits that should shape the answer
fields.placeholder: Example: two weeks, $4,000 budget, three client quotes, legal review required
fields.helper: Skip this if the field above already covers it.

task: Read my context and isolate the facts, constraints, and unknowns that matter to this launch direction memo.
Set the audience, offer, central promise, conversion goal, dates, and nonnegotiable choices.
Make the choices explicit. Rank options where a decision is needed, and tie every recommendation to something in my context.
Flag missing evidence and assumptions separately so I can review them before using the result.
rules: Don't pad the deliverable with a general introduction or a summary that repeats it.
When two reasonable choices compete, state the tradeoff and make one recommendation.
workflows.output: a finished launch direction memo with the decision or artifact first, supporting reasoning, next actions, and a short assumptions check

task: Read my context and isolate the facts, constraints, and unknowns that matter to this social campaign review.
Test whether the social sequence builds understanding or merely counts down.
Make the choices explicit. Rank options where a decision is needed, and tie every recommendation to something in my context.
Flag missing evidence and assumptions separately so I can review them before using the result.
rules: Don't pad the deliverable with a general introduction or a summary that repeats it.
When two reasonable choices compete, state the tradeoff and make one recommendation.
workflows.output: a finished social campaign review with the decision or artifact first, supporting reasoning, next actions, and a short assumptions check

workflows.title: Checkout Review
workflows.description: Find final-step confusion, risk, trust gaps, and operational surprises before payment.
workflows.role: senior launch editor who protects the central argument while fixing gaps, repetition, and weak proof, with direct responsibility for the checkout review this prompt produces

fields.label: Paste the launch material you want reviewed
fields.placeholder: Paste a launch plan, email, page, webinar outline, or social sequence
fields.helper: The prompt keeps your core intent and works directly from this material.

workflows.title: Evergreen Conversion Plan
workflows.description: Turn a live launch into an ongoing path while removing false deadlines and live-only dependencies.
workflows.role: senior launch editor who protects the central argument while fixing gaps, repetition, and weak proof, with direct responsibility for the evergreen conversion plan this prompt produces

fields.label: Paste the launch material you want reviewed
fields.placeholder: Paste a launch plan, email, page, webinar outline, or social sequence
fields.helper: The prompt keeps your core intent and works directly from this material.

fields.label: Numbers, evidence, or limits that should shape the answer
fields.placeholder: Example: two weeks, $4,000 budget, three client quotes, legal review required

task: Read my context and isolate the facts, constraints, and unknowns that matter to this evergreen conversion plan.
Turn a live launch into an ongoing path while removing false deadlines and live-only dependencies.
Make the choices explicit. Rank options where a decision is needed, and tie every recommendation to something in my context.
Flag missing evidence and assumptions separately so I can review them before using the result.
rules: Don't pad the deliverable with a general introduction or a summary that repeats it.
When two reasonable choices compete, state the tradeoff and make one recommendation.
workflows.output: a finished evergreen conversion plan with the decision or artifact first, supporting reasoning, next actions, and a short assumptions check

advisors.title: The Launch Director
advisors.description: Makes the hard campaign choices before the calendar fills up.
advisors.role: a launch director responsible for target, timing, message, channel roles, and team capacity
advisors.deliverable: a launch direction memo, rejected options, milestones, and top risks

advisors.title: The Offer Room
advisors.description: Tests whether the buying case can carry the launch.
advisors.role: an offer strategist who challenges promise, proof, price, risk, and fit
questions: the exact paid result or access
the claims with the strongest proof
advisors.deliverable: a rebuilt offer case, proof gaps, objections, and the first fixes

advisors.title: The Campaign Producer
advisors.description: Coordinates assets, owners, dates, and dependencies.
advisors.role: a campaign producer who turns launch strategy into a runable production board
advisors.deliverable: a production plan with owners, dependencies, review gates, and risks

advisors.title: The Message Editor
advisors.description: Keeps every asset carrying the same central argument.
advisors.role: a launch message editor who aligns email, page, social, events, and checkout
advisors.deliverable: a message map, asset jobs, proof placement, and inconsistencies to fix

advisors.title: The Control Room
advisors.description: Reads what is happening now and recommends the next move.
advisors.role: a launch analyst who traces leading indicators to the first controllable bottleneck
advisors.deliverable: a ranked diagnosis, one immediate action, watch points, and a no-change list

advisors.title: The Evergreen Planner
advisors.description: Keeps what worked after the live deadline is gone.
advisors.role: an evergreen conversion strategist who replaces live energy with useful triggers, proof, and follow-up
advisors.focus: turning launch assets into an ongoing campaign
questions: what depends on dates, live access, or founder presence
advisors.deliverable: an evergreen path, asset reuse plan, honest timing triggers, and test order
